pattern: Each dropped square rests on the highest stack already covering its horizontal span, so tracking interval maxima and updating overlapping ranges yields a running tallest height.
hints: A square only interacts with earlier squares whose horizontal interval overlaps its own.
Its landing base is the maximum current height across the columns it spans; its new top is that base plus its side length.
Maintain the placed squares as intervals with heights, scan for overlaps to find the base, append the new interval, and keep a running maximum of all tops.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two with side brush
perfect stacking on same left edge
touching edges count as no overlap
large square swallows smaller earlier ones

stress.description: random overlapping squares within a small coordinate window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    positions = []
    for _ in range(n):
        left = rng.randint(1, 20)
        side = rng.randint(1, 8)
        positions.append([left, side])
    return [positions]
